params.name: Treatise on the Great
params.description:
On Nov 11, 401 AD, Kumarajiva arrived in Chang'an after a long journey and began to focus on translating Mahayana Buddhist classics such as the Treatise on the Great.
It is a Buddhist classic written by the great monk Nagarjuna and translated by Kumarajiva. The translation consists of as many as 100 volumes, which quote extensively from original Buddhist scriptures and provide detailed explanations of Buddhist concepts, ideas, and history. It can be regarded as a Buddhist encyclopedia and has …
